### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf10481-invoice`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Download Source File

### Download Source File

GET ={{ $json.fileUrl }} — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Parse CSV Contents

### Parse CSV Contents

Parses the incoming CSV binary into JSON items.

Next: Clean & Reshape Rows

### Clean & Reshape Rows

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming invoice records to a stable schema

Next: Load Rows into Notion

### Load Rows into Notion

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Sort Invoices Newest First

### Sort Invoices Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Guard Non-Empty amount

### Guard Non-Empty amount

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Cap Batch Volume

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 100 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Send Summary to Gmail

### Send Summary to Gmail

Sends an email via Gmail — subject is the first line of the message body, recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2

# Issue contracts with HTTP / REST API and Notion without manual work

**Pattern:** Document Generation · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Issue contracts with HTTP / REST API and Notion without manual work. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual reconciliation is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Download Source File → Parse CSV Contents → Clean & Reshape Rows → Load Rows into Notion → Stamp Processing Time → Sort Invoices Newest First → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to document generation without manual effort
- Anyone automating reconciliation in finance
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Standardizes the process every time
- Responds in seconds

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2; Notion integration token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Bad rows are quarantined into their own store, not dropped silently and not allowed to break the whole batch — one malformed row in a 10,000-row file shouldn't stop the other 9,999 from loading, but it also shouldn't vanish without a trace.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Feed it a file with a few deliberately malformed rows and confirm they land in quarantine, not the main store.
2. Switch the source format (e.g. csv to xlsx) and see how little actually has to change.
3. Turn on archive mode and confirm the processed file gets repacked and stored.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your reconciliation process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
